rule_metadata.author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
rule_metadata.title: Unusual or Suspicious Process Ancestry - Command Shell

rules.rule_text:
title: Unusual or Suspicious Process Ancestry - Command Shell
id: 64d04088-5ed5-4125-9219-4bbedec13e9f
status: experimental
description: "We have a lot of detection analytics that seek out suspicious or unusual\
  \ process \nlineage spawning or spawning from cmd.exe. Many of them don\u2019t often\
  \ generate confirmed \nthreat detections but can occasionally raise the flag on\
  \ important threats, like \nExchange compromises. One semi-common pattern in our\
  \ library of analytics is suspicious \nprocess interactions between the Windows\
  \ IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) and the command \nshell. The following amalgamation\
  \ of analytics might help you detect a diverse array of \nmalicious activity related\
  \ to web server compromises. Part of the RedCanary 2024 Threat \nDetection Report.\n"
references:
- https://redcanary.com/threat-detection-report/techniques/windows-command-shell/
author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
date: 2024/03/21
tags:
- attack.execution
- attack.t1059
- attack.t1059.003
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: \cmd.exe
    ParentCommandLine|contains:
    - http://
    - https://
    - echo
    Image|endswith: \powershell.exe
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
- Unknown
level: low
